verses.text: The prophet prays, 'O LORD, I have heard thy speech, and was afraid'; revive thy work, and 'in wrath remember mercy.' Reverent pleading. Even fearing God's judgment, faith asks for mercy in the midst of it.

The prophet prays, 'O LORD, I have heard thy speech, and was afraid'; revive thy work, and 'in wrath remember mercy.' Reverent pleading. Even fearing God's judgment, faith asks for mercy in the midst of it.
God came from Teman, His glory covering the heavens, His brightness as light, plague going before Him. Majestic advent. The prophet pictures God coming in awesome power.
God came from Teman, His glory covering the heavens, His brightness as light, plague going before Him. Majestic advent. The prophet pictures God coming in awesome power.
God came from Teman, His glory covering the heavens, His brightness as light, plague going before Him. Majestic advent. The prophet pictures God coming in awesome power.
He stood and measured the earth; the everlasting mountains were scattered; 'his ways are everlasting.' Unchanging God. The hills crumble, but His ways endure for ever.
He stood and measured the earth; the everlasting mountains were scattered; 'his ways are everlasting.' Unchanging God. The hills crumble, but His ways endure for ever.
Riding His 'horses of salvation,' He cleaves the rivers; the mountains see Him and tremble. God for His people. His power is unleashed to save the ones He loves.
Riding His 'horses of salvation,' He cleaves the rivers; the mountains see Him and tremble. God for His people. His power is unleashed to save the ones He loves.
Riding His 'horses of salvation,' He cleaves the rivers; the mountains see Him and tremble. God for His people. His power is unleashed to save the ones He loves.
Sun and moon stand still as He marches 'for the salvation of thy people,' wounding the head of the wicked. Deliverance. God goes forth to crush the enemy and rescue His own.
Sun and moon stand still as He marches 'for the salvation of thy people,' wounding the head of the wicked. Deliverance. God goes forth to crush the enemy and rescue His own.
Sun and moon stand still as He marches 'for the salvation of thy people,' wounding the head of the wicked. Deliverance. God goes forth to crush the enemy and rescue His own.
He strikes the foe with his own weapons and walks 'through the sea' with His horses. Self-defeating evil. God turns the enemy's strength against himself.
He strikes the foe with his own weapons and walks 'through the sea' with His horses. Self-defeating evil. God turns the enemy's strength against himself.
When the prophet heard, his belly trembled, yet 'I... will rest in the day of trouble.' Trembling trust. Awe at God's power steadies the soul to wait quietly.

'Although the fig tree shall not blossom... yet I will rejoice in the LORD'; He is my strength and makes my feet like hinds' feet. Triumphant faith. Joy in God can stand even when every earthly comfort fails.
